title: Luffy Challenges Krieg’s Idea of Strength
description: On pages 5–8, the fight against Krieg reaches a moral breaking point as Gin, Sanji, Zeff, and Luffy clash over debt, survival, and strength.
scene: Krieg’s weapons and cruelty force everyone at the Baratie to reveal what they truly value: victory, loyalty, survival, or protecting someone else’s dream.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: The final result is what decides who's strong and who's not.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: I'm still standing here so don't you start whining for nothing.

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: his title "Don" as the fleet Admiral of so ships isn't just for show.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Who do you think is more fit to become the pirate King? You or me...?

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: Hey! Don't Egg him on any further!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: It's time to teach that dreamer brat over there,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: If it's just a cannonball, I'll just bounce it back you know!

dialogue.speaker: Don Krieg
dialogue.text: please, Don! Allow me to kill this man with my own H...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Whether you kill him or I do, the result is the same.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: You know that there's nothing I hate more,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: than foolishly naive things like "honour" or "compassion."

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Listen well. This is a battle we're in.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: And in a battle, you fight to win.

summary: This part pushes the Baratie battle toward its climax by contrasting Krieg’s ruthless power with Luffy and Sanji’s stubborn ideals.
